summary: End-to-end testing of Çince→Türkçe Dublaj App. All 11 backend pytest cases passed (8.5s) and all critical frontend flows passed via Playwright. Real pipeline ran (Whisper transcription + deep-translator + edge-tts + ffmpeg) with a generated 10s Chinese MP4 — produced a valid dubbed MP4 (video+audio streams verified by ffprobe) with 1 transcribed/translated segment. UI is fully Turkish, dark theme, segments rendered side-by-side, history populates and delete works.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses BackgroundTasks + a sync pymongo client inside _process_job_sync — works but holds an OS thread per active job; for 2-5 minute videos under concurrent load this can starve the FastAPI thread pool. Consider a queue (e.g. RQ/Celery/asyncio.Queue + worker process).
ALLOWED_EXT defined in server.py but the same list is duplicated client-side in DubbingStudio.jsx (`.mp4/.mov/.m4v/.webm/.mkv`). Risk of drift; expose via /api/voices-style endpoint or /api/config.
delete_job() iterates ALLOWED_EXT to find the upload — fine, but the pipeline only stores extension at upload time; better to persist `upload_path` on the Job document and unlink directly.
/api/job/{id}/download has no auth and uses raw job_id from URL — acceptable for keyless app, but FileResponse should validate that out file path stays inside OUTPUT_DIR (path traversal hardening).
DubbingStudio.jsx polls every 1500 ms with no exponential backoff and never stops if the tab regains focus after error — minor; fine for current scope.

demucs fallback in dubbing_pipeline.py logs a warning then writes original audio as `vocals` and -10 dB copy as `music` — acceptable per requirements, but the resulting 'separated' tracks are essentially the same source, which causes the original Chinese voice to remain audible under the Turkish dub. Worth surfacing in UI when fallback was used.

translate_segments() catches any GoogleTranslator exception and silently sets text_tr='' — failures are not surfaced in the job document; consider attaching warnings to job.message or job.warnings list.
Whisper model is loaded lazily on first request — first job has higher latency. Consider warming the model at app startup.
seed_data_creation: Generated /tmp/test.mp4 (10 s, 320x240, Chinese TTS audio 你好世界，今天天气很好) for upload testing. Created/deleted real job records during pipeline runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test video at /tmp/test.mp4. Whisper 'base' model already cached at ~/.cache/whisper so subsequent pipeline runs complete in ~3-5s. Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_dubbing_api.py — run via `pytest backend/tests/ -v`. Frontend external URL = https://autosync-dub.preview.emergentagent.com. Polling can transition from 5% directly to 100% in observation window because the pipeline is very fast on this short test clip; use a longer video if step-by-step stage transitions need to be visually verified.
